rules.description: Indicates the billing method for the shipment identifying who pays for shipping charges (Prepaid, Free Domicile, or Freight Collect).
Indicates the billing method for the shipment such as Prepaid (P/P), as specified in the UPS Guide to Labeling.
Routing instruction indicator that must print on the label when shipping biological substances.
The Consolidated Movement Shipment ID number for UPS Trade Direct shipments, consisting of 9 numeric digits plus a check digit.
A general description of the contents or commodities being shipped, required for international shipments.
Indicator identifying the type of international documentation accompanying the shipment.
Indicates whether UPS Operations needs to perform an acceptance audit of a dry ice package.
Mandatory declaration text for dry ice shipments including UN number, proper shipping name, hazard class, and weight in kilograms.
Electronic Export Information indicator required when UPS is completing AES filing for State Department License shipments.
The shipment's customs clearance number calculated from the virtual document box 1Z tracking number, used on summary labels.
UPS import site information block for World Ease shipments containing the import site port name, country, and postal code.
Text indicator alerting UPS Operations about the documentation status of an international shipment, particularly for paperless shipments.
The AES Internal Transaction Number assigned when the shipper has completed AES filing, which takes precedence over reference numbers.
MaxiCode 2D barcode on World Ease over-labels encoding postal code, country code, service class, tracking number, and routing data.
2D MaxiCode barcode encoding shipment information including tracking number, class of service, and routing data for automated sorting.
A 3-digit numeric code encoded within the MaxiCode barcode identifying the specific UPS service and options for the shipment.
The 3-letter airport or facility code for the origin location, used on Air Freight labels.
Indicates the package number relative to the total number of packages in the shipment.
The weight of the individual package with unit of measure, format varying by service type and origin country.
PDF417 2D barcode required for international paperless shipments and certain exchange service labels containing shipment data.
Package count indicator showing the package number within the total shipment in 'N OF X' format.
Power of Attorney / Country of Origin indicator appearing on World Ease labels when applicable.
Power of Attorney routing instruction indicator required when shipper authorizes UPS to complete AES filing.
Port of entry information containing UPS import site port name, country name, and postal code for World Ease shipments.
Postal barcode reflecting UPS import site information, printed to the right of MaxiCode on World Ease over-labels.
Refrigeration handling indicator for packages requiring refrigeration, often used with UPS Premier service.
UPS postal routing code consisting of country/state ISO code, three-digit postal zone, and sort code suffix.
State Department License indicator alerting UPS Operations that the shipment contains export-controlled articles.
Short alphanumeric code representing the UPS service level used internally for routing and processing.
Visual icon or text code representing the service level for quick visual identification by UPS Operations.
Two-character alphanumeric code embedded within the tracking number (positions 8-9) identifying the UPS service and options.
The name of the UPS service printed prominently on the label identifying the level of delivery service.
Identifies the UPS service level for the shipment, including World Ease and Trade Direct designations.
The UPS service level displayed as a text banner on the label indicating the speed and type of delivery service.
The date the shipment is tendered to UPS, formatted as DD MMM YYYY.
The complete shipper/origin address block containing contact name, phone, company, street, city, state, postal code, and country.
The city, state/province, and postal/ZIP code of the shipping origin location.
The name of the company or business from which the shipment originates.
The name of the contact person at the shipping origin/sender location.
The country of the shipping origin, required for international shipments, displayed as full country name.
Additional address information for the shipper such as suite number, floor, or building name.
The telephone number of the shipper/sender contact in the ship-from address block.
The city, state/province, and postal code line for the shipper's address.
The street address of the shipping origin/sender location.
The full consignee/destination address block containing recipient name, phone, company, street, city, state, postal code, and country.
The city, state/province, and postal/ZIP code of the delivery destination.
The name of the company or business at the delivery destination.
The contact name of the consignee/recipient at the destination.
The country of the delivery destination, displayed for international shipments.
Additional address information for the delivery destination such as suite number, floor, or building name.
The telephone number of the consignee/recipient contact at the delivery destination.
The destination city, state/province, and postal code for the consignee, including USPS postal code for SurePost.
The street address of the consignee/recipient at the destination.
The date the shipment was tendered, displayed in DD MMM YYYY format with a DATE: prefix.
The dimensional (volumetric) weight of the shipment used for billing when it exceeds actual weight.
A unique identifier for the overall shipment linking all packages in a multi-piece shipment together.
The total actual weight of the shipment rounded to the next whole pound or kilogram.
Instructions on the summary label directing which package the label should be applied to.
The UPS account/shipper number identifying the shipper's UPS account.
Total number of packages in a consolidated shipment, shown on summary labels in 'TOTAL PKG #:' or 'PKG/PLT #:' format
UPS 1Z tracking number, 18 alphanumeric characters uniquely identifying each package
Last three digits of the 1Z tracking number printed on World Ease over-labels and summary labels
Modified MOD-10 check digit at position 19 of the UPS Trade Direct Sub Pro Number
19-character alphanumeric identifier for UPS Trade Direct shipments including MOD-10 check digit
Unique Shipment Identifier with 9 numeric digits plus a check digit, required for Express Freight and SurePost labels
White 'V' character inside a black square indicating a UPS Premier shipment
Three-character code indicating UPS Premier service level: Silver, Gold, or Platinum
UPS routing code for the import site, used on World Ease labels alongside MaxiCode or in the import site box
34-digit USPS eVS tracking number starting with 9, used for final-mile delivery on UPS SurePost shipments
